verses.1: Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth,
verses.2: Before the sun, the light, the moon, and the stars are darkened,
verses.3: in the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble,
verses.4: and the doors shall be shut in the street;
verses.5: yes, they shall be afraid of heights,
verses.6: before the silver cord is severed,
verses.7: and the dust returns to the earth as it was,
verses.9: Further, because the Preacher was wise, he still taught the people knowledge. Yes, he pondered, sought out, and set in order many proverbs.
verses.10: The Preacher sought to find out acceptable words, and that which was written blamelessly, words of truth.
verses.11: The words of the wise are like goads; and like nails well fastened are words from the masters of assemblies, which are given from one shepherd.
verses.12: Furthermore, my son, be admonished: of making many books there is no end; and much study is a weariness of the flesh.
verses.13: This is the end of the matter. All has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments; for this is the whole duty of man.
verses.14: For God will bring every work into judgment, with every hidden thing, whether it is good, or whether it is evil.
